facts.text: Atlanta earned its name in 1845 when the Western and Atlantic Railroad christened the town, replacing the earlier moniker Marthasville.
Atlanta grew from a railroad terminus into a bustling junction where multiple lines met, sparking rapid expansion.
Atlanta stole the world spotlight in 1996 by hosting the Summer Olympics, a move that reshaped the city’s skyline and economy.
Georgia’s tax credit turned Atlanta into a film capital, injecting $9.5 billion in 2017 and housing studios like Tyler Perry and Pinewood.
The Allman Brothers’ “Hot ’Lanta” and Lynyrd Skynyrd’s “Free Bird” echo Atlanta’s legendary Southern rock scene.
The National Center for Civil and Human Rights in Atlanta explores the civil rights movement and its global human rights impact.
